claim: Spec-to-code workflows (write spec, run compiler, iterate) produce progressively worse code and are just 'vibe coding by another name'; the problem is ignoring code structure while iterating
actionable: Never use spec-to-code loops without reviewing and refactoring code between iterations; always maintain code quality checks
source_title: Software Fundamentals Matter More Than Ever

claim: Bad code is harder to change; good codebases are easy to change. AI works better on good codebases because it can understand and extend them without introducing bugs
actionable: Invest in keeping your codebase well-structured with clear modules; this multiplies the value of AI agents working on it
source_title: Software Fundamentals Matter More Than Ever

claim: The 'Grill Me' skill forces AI to interview you with 40-100 questions until reaching shared understanding of requirements before building, replacing vague spec handoffs
actionable: Use the 'Grill Me' skill to interview requirements deeply before delegating to AI agents; capture the design concept, not just surface requirements
source_title: Software Fundamentals Matter More Than Ever

claim: Using a ubiquitous language (markdown file of shared domain terms) aligns AI and humans; this makes AI think less verbosely and produces more aligned implementations
actionable: Create a ubiquitous language markdown file listing domain terms, keep it open during planning, pass it to AI agents to ensure terminology alignment
source_title: Software Fundamentals Matter More Than Ever

claim: Deep modules (lots of functionality, simple interface) are far more testable and easier for AI to navigate than shallow modules (little functionality, complex interface)
actionable: Design code with deep modules having simple, well-controlled interfaces; refactor shallow module hierarchies to improve AI comprehension
source_title: Software Fundamentals Matter More Than Ever

claim: Test-driven development (TDD) forces AI agents to take small deliberate steps and use feedback loops effectively; AI by default does too much at once before testing
actionable: Enforce TDD workflows with AI agents: write tests first, make them pass, then refactor; this prevents AI from accumulating too many untested changes
source_title: Software Fundamentals Matter More Than Ever

claim: Design the interface, delegate the implementation: you as the engineer design module boundaries and interfaces, AI handles implementation details; this saves cognitive load
actionable: Define clear module interfaces in your PRDs; delegate implementation to AI; review/test at the boundary rather than inside modules
source_title: Software Fundamentals Matter More Than Ever
